text:
Listen — a terrible week is just that: a week. It is a small, ugly box of time, and it does not get to define you or predict what comes next. Right now it feels like the whole sky has fallen in, but weeks are not weather systems that settle in permanently; they pass, they always pass, and the version of you reading this in a month will barely remember the specific weight of today. That's not me being dismissive. It's just true that almost nothing feels as enormous in the rearview mirror as it does in the windshield.

Here's what I want you to hold onto. The fact that this week wore you down means you've been carrying something real, and carrying hard things is not a sign that you're failing — it's a sign that you're strong enough to still be standing here, tired and frustrated and reaching for a little encouragement instead of giving up. That instinct, the one that made you want to feel better, is the same instinct that's going to get you through. It hasn't gone anywhere.

So be gentle with yourself tonight. Lower the bar to something humane: a glass of water, a real meal, getting horizontal at a reasonable hour, one small thing that is just for you and not for anyone's approval. You don't have to fix everything or understand everything or win anything right now. You just have to get to tomorrow, and you are extraordinarily good at getting to tomorrow — you've done it every single day of your life so far, a perfect record.

The week will end. You will not. And the people and things worth your energy will still be there on the other side of it, waiting for the rested, recovered version of you. Hang in there. You're doing better than you think.
